title: Action Plan: Edinburgh Historical Weather via UK Met Office DataHub (Filesystem JSON first)
goal: Integrate UK Met Office DataHub for Edinburgh-focused historical weather and store normalized hourly JSON files on disk, with clear handling of auth, limits, and retries.

must_be_confirmed_before_build: Met Office DataHub account registration completed.
API key/credentials provisioned and validated in non-production environment.
Selected product/endpoints for hourly historical data confirmed.
Free-tier quota/limit expectations documented (request caps, window limits).
Final variable list, units, and date range approved.
Filesystem path, run schedule, and error handling policy approved.
credentials_and_access: Met Office DataHub API credentials are required (store in environment variables, never in repo).
Network access to Met Office endpoints must be allowed from runtime environment.

included: Met Office DataHub authentication and client setup
Hourly data normalization and JSON file persistence

phases.name: Upfront Requirements Gate
tasks: Complete all credential and access prerequisites from upfront_requirements.
Run a manual API smoke test with real credentials and save response sample.
Document quota assumptions and backoff plan before coding ingestion.
Block implementation until definition_of_ready_checklist is complete.

phases.name: Overview and Scope
tasks: Confirm Met Office endpoint(s) for historical/hourly access relevant to Edinburgh.
Define required account setup and credential handling process.
Define target weather fields and canonical local schema.
Define expected free-tier constraints and fetch schedule.

phases.name: Filesystem Data Layer
tasks: Create storage path (for example data/weather/met-office/edinburgh).
Define monthly JSON file contract and manifest file.
Define sync-state file with window cursor, retry counters, and last success timestamp.
Implement deterministic file naming and safe write semantics.

phases.name: Backend Integration Layer
tasks: Implement Met Office API client with auth token/header support.
Implement throttled window fetcher respecting provider limits.
Normalize provider-specific units/fields into canonical shape.
Implement dedupe merge by UTC timestamp and rewrite changed rows only.
Implement fallback strategy for intermittent provider errors (retry and skip-window marker).

phases.name: API and UX Layer
tasks: Add trigger endpoint for backfill and incremental update.
Add status endpoint exposing quota usage, last successful window, and pending windows.
Add read endpoint returning stored weather JSON slices by date range.
Keep UI minimal and focused on sync controls/status.

phases.name: Testing
tasks: Unit tests for auth handling, field mapping, and unit conversions.
Integration tests with mocked Met Office responses and real filesystem writes.
Integration tests for resume behavior after forced mid-run interruption.
E2E test for trigger/status flow in local app.

phases.name: Documentation
tasks: Document registration/auth setup and local env configuration.
Document endpoint/limit assumptions and mitigation approach.
Document canonical JSON schema and checkpoint semantics.

phases.name: Deployment and Verification
tasks: Run quality gates and validate no secret leakage in logs.
Perform first historical pull and verify sample windows.
Confirm rerun idempotency and checkpoint correctness.
Record deltas versus Open-Meteo for future source comparison.

notes: Met Office path is designed for higher-trust UK workflows.
Auth and quota handling are first-class concerns in this plan.
